name: Logz.io API
what_it_does: Managed ELK/OpenSearch platform (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) providing hosted log management, infrastructure monitoring, distributed tracing, and SIEM capabilities via REST API.
use_cases: Running Elasticsearch/OpenSearch queries against log data from agent workflows
Managing log shipping configurations and Logstash pipelines
Querying traces and spans for distributed system debugging
Creating and managing alerting rules for anomaly detection
Exporting log data for compliance or long-term archival workflows
not_for: Teams not using Logz.io or preferring self-managed ELK
Real-time streaming log processing (Logz.io is analytics, not streaming)
Teams preferring Datadog or Sumo Logic ecosystem
best_when: Your team uses Logz.io for managed ELK and you need agents to query log data, manage alerting, or integrate observability data into automated workflows.
avoid_when: You prefer self-managed Elasticsearch or other managed observability platforms.

auth.notes: API token with account-level access. Separate tokens for log shipping (Logstash token) and management API. Tokens are long-lived with no expiration by default.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro plans starting from ~$87/month; pricing based on daily log volume
pricing.notes: Log volume-based pricing. Enterprise plans for large deployments. No permanent free tier. Trial provides full feature access.

performance.rate_limits: API: varies by endpoint; typically 10-100 requests/minute

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP errors with JSON body. Elasticsearch query errors surfaced from underlying engine. Error messages are generally descriptive for query syntax issues.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alert and saved search creation is not idempotent. Log queries are read-only and inherently idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API token scopes are account-wide — separate tokens for different environments or teams
Elasticsearch DSL query syntax knowledge is required for log searches
Log data retention depends on your plan tier — queries beyond retention period return no results
Logz.io region determines the API endpoint URL (US vs EU)
Sub-accounts have separate API tokens from the main account
Some UI features (dashboards, visualizations) are not exposed via API
